boards.description: Build a short (CVC) word by choosing its sounds in order.

item.name: (word)
item.note: blendWords is a flat list of decodable words

boards.description: Fill the blank in a sentence using context clues — pick the word that makes sense.
Two letters, one sound: pick the digraph team (sh, ch, th, ck) that spells the sound.
Figurative language: a reminder, then identify or interpret the figure of speech (similes, metaphors, idioms). Shares the RuleQuiz interaction but draws from the figurative collection.

item.name: rule
item.example: a simile compares using like or as

item.name: question
item.example: 'as busy as a bee' is a…

boards.description: Find the Detail: read a short story, then answer a spoken wh-question whose answer is stated in the text. Renders via the StoryReader board.

item.name: story
item.example: The cat is on the mat. The cat is big.

item.name: q
item.note: rq-* clip key for the spoken question

boards.description: First Readers: read along to a short decodable story (narrated word by word), then tap the word you heard. Renders via the StoryReader board.

item.name: story
item.example: The cat is on the mat. The cat can nap.

boards.description: Spot the describing word (adjective). Draws from the adjectives array of the parts_of_speech word bank.

Apply a grammar rule: a reminder, then pick the correct form (subject–verb agreement, pronouns, irregular past, sentence types). Shares the RuleQuiz interaction but draws from the grammar_depth collection.

item.name: rule
item.example: add -s to the verb for he/she/it
item.note: the rule shown as the reminder banner

boards.description: Spot the naming word (noun) — sorts words by part of speech. Draws from the nouns array of the parts_of_speech word bank.

item.name: (word)
item.note: tagged noun in the word bank

boards.description: Spot the doing word (verb). Draws from the verbs array of the parts_of_speech word bank.
Read Between the Lines: read a short story, then infer the answer to a spoken wh-question using clues. Renders via the StoryReader board.

item.name: story
item.example: The dog can run and jump. The dog can dig.

item.name: q
item.note: rq-* clip key for the spoken question

boards.description: Main Idea: read a short story, then answer a spoken wh-question about what it is about. Renders via the StoryReader board.

item.name: story
item.example: The cat sat on the mat. The cat can nap. The cat is little.

item.name: q
item.note: rq-* clip key for the spoken question

boards.description: Match a picture to its written word (or vice versa). Core vocabulary recognition. Shows an optional native-language gloss (ru) beneath the word for foreign-language courses.
Choose the right end mark for a sentence (. ? !).
Apply a spelling/grammar rule: a reminder of the rule, then pick the word that follows it (Bossy R, Magic E, i-before-e…).

item.name: rule
item.example: i before e, except after c
item.note: the mnemonic shown as the reminder banner

item.name: answer
item.note: the word that follows the rule

item.name: distractors
item.note: wrong-but-plausible choices that break the rule

boards.description: Same or opposite? Pick the synonym or the antonym of the given word. Draws from both the synonyms and antonyms collections.
Build a correct sentence by ordering the words (capital first, period last).

item.name: (sentence)
item.note: a model sentence the words are drawn from

boards.description: Build an English sentence from scrambled word tiles, prompted by its native-language meaning; each tile speaks its word. Foreign-language sentence construction (renders via SentenceBuilder).

item.name: en
item.note: the target English sentence (words scrambled into tiles)

item.name: ru
item.note: native-language meaning shown as the prompt

boards.description: Choose the high-frequency sight word that was spoken — words you learn by sight, not sounding out.

Advanced phonics: a reminder of a decoding pattern (vowel team, r-controlled, diphthong, silent letter), then pick the word that uses it. Shares the RuleQuiz interaction but draws from the patterns collection.

item.name: rule
item.example: ai and ay spell long A
item.note: the pattern shown as the reminder banner

item.name: question
item.example: Which spells the long A sound?

item.name: answer
item.note: the word that uses the pattern

boards.description: Hear a sound, tap the letter that makes it. The first phonics interaction.
Spelling Bee: a reminder tip, then pick the correctly-spelled word (tricky high-frequency words, homophones, word endings). Shares the RuleQuiz interaction but draws from the spellings collection.

item.name: rule
item.note: the spelling tip shown as the reminder banner

boards.description: Hear a vocabulary word, tap it among choices — the listening counterpart of picture-match. Reuses the vocab bank (incl. the optional ru gloss); ideal for foreign-language listening.

Build a word form by tapping its parts in order (walk+ed → walked, un+happy → unhappy). Teaches morphology: verb tenses, plurals, comparatives, prefixes & suffixes. The tray mixes correct parts with distractor tiles; press Check when assembled.

item.name: rule
item.note: the rule shown as the reminder banner

item.name: parts
item.note: the ordered correct tiles (joined with no space = answer)

item.name: distractors
item.note: extra tiles the child must reject

boards.description: Word families: same ending, swap the first sound (-at → cat, hat, mat).

views.description: Sounds blending into a word: letter chips appear one by one, then resolve into the whole word ("c-a-t → cat").
A sentence with a blank (___), optionally shown filled with the answer. For context-clue / fill-in beats.

fields.name: sentence
fields.note: must contain "___" where the blank goes

fields.name: answer
fields.note: omit to show the blank empty; set to reveal it

views.description: A category label plus a few example words — used to teach a part of speech (naming/doing/describing words).
A row of letter tiles; the active one lifts and glows. Good for intro/recap beats that point at a set of letters.

fields.name: active
fields.note: index of the highlighted letter; -1 for none

views.description: Two words in a relationship: same meaning (=) or opposites (⇄), colour-coded with a label.
One letter in focus: a big letter card plus an example word and picture, with the first letter coloured.
A picture above its word — the core vocabulary visual (see it, read it).
A full sentence with the capital first letter and the end punctuation highlighted — for building/punctuation beats.

fields.name: text
fields.note: include the capital and end mark; both get highlighted

views.description: Interactive sound wall: every letter and digraph as a tappable tile — tap one to hear its sound. A whole-alphabet "explore it yourself" screen.
Two letters teaming up to make one sound (a digraph), shown as letters = sound, then an example word + picture.

content.description: Letter → its sound and an example word. Feeds the sound-to-letter board and phoneme lessons.

item.name: band
item.note: phonics band this letter is introduced in (0=s,a,t,p,i,n; 1=+m,d,g,o,c,k; 2=all)

content.description: Two-letter teams that make one sound (sh, ch, th, ck).
Vocabulary words paired with a picture (emoji), plus an optional native-language gloss (ru/…) for foreign-language courses. Feeds picture-match + listen-from-vocab.

item.name: ru
item.note: optional native-language gloss shown beneath the word (EFL courses)

item.name: band
item.note: optional difficulty/topic band; a station selects its slice via bands:

content.description: Decodable CVC words for sound-blending, tagged with the BLEND_BANDS band they are introduced in.

item.name: band
item.note: first BLEND_BANDS array the word appears in

content.description: Word families grouped by rime (-at → cat, hat, mat). Feeds the word-family board.

item.name: band
item.note: difficulty band the family belongs to

content.description: Word pairs that mean the same. Feeds the same/opposite board (same mode).
Word pairs that are opposites. Feeds the same/opposite board (opposite mode).
Cloze sentences with the answer + distractors. Feeds the context-clue board.
High-frequency words learned by sight (not sounded out).
The grammar word bank, role-keyed: nouns / verbs / adjectives. The generator draws from the appropriate array per category.
Model sentences for the sentence-builder board (capital first, period last).

English sentences paired with a native-language meaning (ru) for foreign-language sentence-building. The learner reads the native-language prompt and orders the scrambled English word tiles to build the English sentence.

item.name: en
item.note: the target English sentence (words are scrambled into tiles)

item.name: ru
item.note: native-language meaning shown as the prompt

item.name: band
item.note: optional difficulty/topic band; a station selects its slice via bands:

item.note: the spelling tip shown as the reminder banner

item.note: the rule shown as the reminder banner

item.note: extra tiles the child must reject

content.description: Figurative-language items: a reminder + a question identifying or interpreting a figure of speech (similes, metaphors, idioms). Feeds the figurative board. Same shape as rules.

item.example: a simile compares using like or as

content.description: Sentences with no end mark; the punctuation board asks which of . ? ! fits.
First Readers (recognition): a short decodable story, the target word to hear/tap (q), and word distractors (d). Feeds the firstReader board (StoryReader).

item.example: The cat is on the mat. The cat can nap.

item.example: The cat sat on the mat. The cat can nap. The cat is little.
item.note: narrated word by word using word-<w> clips

content.description: Find the Detail comprehension: a story + a spoken wh-question (q = rq-* clip key), the displayed question text, the answer (a), and distractors (d). Feeds the findDetail board (StoryReader).

item.name: story
item.example: The cat is on the mat. The cat is big.
item.note: narrated word by word using word-<w> clips

item.note: clip key for the spoken question (a narration rq-* key)

content.description: Inference comprehension: a story + a spoken wh-question (q = rq-* clip key), the displayed question text, the answer (a), and distractors (d). Feeds the inference board (StoryReader).

item.example: The dog can run and jump. The dog can dig.
item.note: narrated word by word using word-<w> clips
